content: Acute ischemic stroke treatment algorithm: (1) Immediate non-contrast CT to exclude hemorrhage. (2) If within 4.5 hours and no contraindications: IV tPA (alteplase 0.9 mg/kg, max 90mg; or tenecteplase 0.25 mg/kg). Contraindications include recent surgery, bleeding diathesis, BP >185/110 despite treatment. (3) For large-vessel occlusion (ICA, M1 MCA, basilar): mechanical thrombectomy up to 6 hours universally, and 6-24 hours with favorable perfusion imaging (mismatch between core and penumbra). (4) Antiplatelet therapy: aspirin 160-325mg within 24-48 hours (delayed 24 hours post-tPA). (5) Secondary prevention: statin therapy, BP control (<130/80), anticoagulation for atrial fibrillation (DOAC preferred), carotid endarterectomy/stenting for symptomatic stenosis >70%. Hemorrhagic stroke management focuses on BP control (SBP 140-160), reversal agents (PCC for warfarin, idarucizumab for dabigatran, andexanet alfa for factor Xa inhibitors), and neurosurgical consultation.
